summary: Frontend-only visual regression check on Checkout Order Summary quantity badges. Seeded localStorage cart with multiple items (qty 1, 3, 12, and on mobile 5/2) and navigated to /checkout. Inspected geometry of every .order-item-qty badge vs its scrollable parent .order-items, took comparison screenshots in default state and with overflow:visible forced. CONFIRMED the user-reported clipping bug is NOT fully fixed: the FIRST item's quantity badge is still clipped by 8px at the top in both desktop (1920x1080) and mobile (390x844) viewports.

ui_bugs.issue: Quantity badge (.order-item-qty) on the FIRST product in the Order Summary list is clipped 8px at the top. Root cause: the parent .order-items has 'overflow-y: auto' (Checkout.css line 247). Per CSS spec, when overflow-y != visible, the computed overflow-x is forced to 'auto' as well, so BOTH axes clip. The badge is absolutely positioned at top:-8px right:-8px relative to .order-item-image, but for the first item the image is flush against the parent's content-box top edge → the upper 8px of the badge is clipped. Subsequent items have the 16px row gap as breathing room so their badges are NOT clipped. The previous fix (removing overflow:hidden on .order-item-image, adding z-index:1 / box-shadow) did NOT solve the root cause because the clipping is on the SCROLL CONTAINER, not on .order-item-image. Confirmed visually: forcing .order-items { overflow: visible } via devtools renders the '3' badge as a complete circle; default state shows only the bottom portion.

issues: First item quantity badge top edge clipped 8px on both desktop and mobile - measurable via getBoundingClientRect (badge.top=397, parent.top=405; parent overflow auto/auto).

Quantity badges 'min-width: 20px; height: 20px' with 'top:-8px; right:-8px' will clip whenever the item is at the visible top of the scrollable container - same problem will resurface when user scrolls within the list.

action_items: Fix the first-item badge clipping in /app/frontend/src/pages/Checkout.css. Recommended options (pick one):

Option A (simplest, safest): Add padding-top: 12px and padding-left/right: 10px to .order-items (line 239-248). This gives the protruding badge room without changing overflow semantics. Keep max-height:300px scrolling behavior intact.

Option B: Remove the protruding badge - place the badge inside the image (top: 4px right: 4px instead of -8px/-8px). Add a subtle white border or shadow for separation. Eliminates clipping entirely on any scroll position.
Option C: Wrap the scrollable list inner and give the outer .order-items overflow:visible; only the inner scroll wrapper has overflow:auto with padding. More invasive.

After fix, retest with 2+ items and verify: (1) first item badge fully visible, (2) when user scrolls inside .order-items the badge of the new top item is still fully visible (recommend Option A combined with Option B for robustness).
critical_code_review_comments: Checkout.css .order-items has overflow-y:auto without padding-top - any absolutely-positioned overflow-protruding child (badge) will be clipped at the top of the visible scroll area. Common UI pitfall.
Behavior is identical at desktop and mobile breakpoints; the @media (max-width:600px) block does not address it.

Cosmetic: .order-item-qty uses font-size:11px with min-width:20px - readable, but the badge for quantities >= 100 (3 digits) will widen but stay within image right edge; verify with a 3-digit test case if business supports >99 qty (likely not, but worth confirming validation).
test_credentials: N/A - cart seeded via localStorage key 'kostin_cart'
seed_data_creation: Seeded localStorage 'kostin_cart' with 2-3 test items (id=test-1..test-3) of varying quantities (1, 3, 12, 5) to exercise badge visibility on /checkout. No backend writes performed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reproduction is purely visual + measurable. Quick repro: open browser console on /checkout, run `localStorage.setItem('kostin_cart', JSON.stringify([{id:'t1',name:'X',image:'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1541643600914-78b084683601?w=200',price:10,stock:5,quantity:3}]))` then reload. Inspect .order-item-qty bounding box vs .order-items bounding box - first item's badge.top will be 8px above parent.top while parent has overflow auto/auto → clipped. Comparison screenshots saved at /app/test_reports/first_item_zoom.png (clipped) vs first_item_overflow_visible.png (full circle).
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE: .order-items (Checkout.css L239-248) sets max-height:300px + overflow-y:auto. CSS spec: when one axis is set to a non-visible overflow value, the other axis's 'visible' computes to 'auto'. So overflow-x is also clipping. .order-item-qty is positioned at top:-8px right:-8px relative to .order-item-image. For the very first .order-item, its image sits flush against the parent's content-box top edge (no padding-top on .order-items), so the 8px of the badge that overflows above is INSIDE the clip region of the scroll container → cut off. Subsequent items have the 16px gap between siblings as breathing room, so their badges fit inside the parent's clip rect. REPRODUCTION: seed cart with >=1 item, navigate to /checkout, inspect first badge - badge.getBoundingClientRect().top is 8px less than .order-items.getBoundingClientRect().top (confirmed: badge top=397, parent top=405; mobile: same delta). MITIGATION: see action_items Option A (add padding-top:12px to .order-items) - simplest, no semantic change.
